attributes.description: Fires when the sum of per-second RATEs for `system.network.errors` and `system.network.dropped` for a host (all interfaces) exceeds 0.1 (combined errors+drops per second). Uses the TS time-series source. Tune the final threshold; use packet-normalised rate in a fork if you need % of traffic.
attributes.name: [System Host Metrics OTel] Network error and drop rate above threshold

investigation_guide.blob:
## Interface errors or drops

1. Per-interface `device` and `direction` on the **Network** dashboard.
2. Coalesce: NIC duplex mismatch, bad cable, or kernel buffer size (`drops`).
3. This roll-up sums all interfaces; for pin-pointing, duplicate the query with `BY` `device`.

**Tune** Set `0.1` in `bad_per_sec > 0.1` — events per second, not a ratio.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-hostmetricsreceiver.otel-*
| STATS e = SUM(RATE(`metrics.system.network.errors`)), dr = SUM(RATE(`metrics.system.network.dropped`)) BY `host.name`
| EVAL bad_per_sec = e + dr
| WHERE bad_per_sec > 0.1
| SORT bad_per_sec DESC
| LIMIT 20
